data.title: Options Corner: Teradyne's Value Proposition Also Bodes Well For Swing Traders
data.description:
With the broader technology sector enjoying a resurgence, several of the usual suspects have benefited tremendously from the momentum spike. Still, Teradyne Inc TER, a leading supplier of automated test equipment (ATE) and industrial automation solutions, has lagged its innovation-centric peers. At the same time, the potential trickle-down effect could lift TER stock, making it both a possible value investment and an intriguing trade.

Recently, the tech-oriented Nasdaq Composite eked into positive territory for the year, symbolizing the resurgence that has taken place among publicly traded innovators. Over the past 52 weeks, the index has now gained over 15%. Terdayne, though, has not shared in the enthusiasm. On a year-to-date basis, TER stock is down more than 34%. Over the past 52 weeks, it has lost nearly 41%.

Nevertheless, there's legitimate cause for optimism. Semiconductor juggernauts like Nvidia Corp NVDA and Advanced Micro Devices Inc AMD have regained their mojo in recent sessions. Part of the enthusiasm could be traced to catalysts such as the "TACO" trade, a crude acronym that stands for ‘Trump Always Chickens Out.'

Essentially, the market is digesting President Donald Trump's tariff policies and accompanying aggressive rhetoric. Initially, the tariffs sparked great concern among investors, leading to severe volatility. However, Trump has a tendency of talking tough first and then walking back the bravado. The market has noticed and is responding accordingly.

Subsequently, concerns about the impact of retaliatory measures against the tech sector has lessened somewhat. Combined with strong earnings results from the semiconductor space, the landscape appears compelling for TER stock. Fundamentally, that's because advanced chips are incredibly complex, requiring rigorous testing protocols. Since this process represents Teradyne's bread and butter, TER presents an intriguing value proposition.

It also might make for a quick trade.

A Sentiment Reversal Could Be Due For TER Stock

To be completely fair, the fundamental argument that Teradyne's behind-the-scenes relevance should boost its business was mentioned last year. As stated earlier, TER stock has lost a massive amount of equity value since that argument was issued. This goes to show how investing narratives — no matter how convincing — aren't necessarily the best predictors.

On the flipside, when it comes to the discipline of trading, predictability runs at the heart of the speculative practice. Traders aren't necessarily focused on the narratives, though they provide important context. Storylines represent the "why" of a target enterprise or asset. Traders are focused on the "how" — how much, how fast, how likely.

In other words, the central theme in options is probability: how likely is it that a particular stock will meet its profitability threshold within an allocated time period? To answer this question, traders should turn to market breadth or the assessment of patterns of accumulation and distribution.

Ordinarily, financial experts will turn to fundamental and technical analysis to aid in their decision-making process. Unfortunately, these methodologies are only partially useful because of their property of non-stationarity. That's the academic way of saying that core metrics such as earnings growth or share price generally fluctuate, especially across vast time periods and sentiment regimes.

For example, TER stock was trading around $20 a share a decade ago. It's above $82 today, meaning that statistical clustering is really only applicable for a narrow time range. In contrast, market breadth is simply demand and demand is a binary construct. Such constructs existed in 2015 and they're applicable today.

In the past two months, TER stock printed a "3-7-D" market breadth sequence: three up weeks, seven down weeks, with a net negative trajectory across the 10-week period. Notably, in 60.71% of cases, the following week's price action results in upside, with a median return of 5.25%. Based on Friday's close of $78.60, TER is on track to hit $82.73, which is now a very realistic target.

Should the bulls maintain control of the market, the median return for the second week stands at 2.75%. If so, that should push TER stock a hair over the $85 level.

Getting Down To Business

Using the above market intelligence, the most rational multi-leg options strategy may be the 80/85 bull call spread expiring June 20. This transaction involves buying the $80 call and simultaneously selling the $85 call, for a net debit paid of $270. Should TER stock rise through the short strike price at expiration, the maximum reward is $230, a payout of over 85%.

Primarily, what makes this trade attractive is the implied shift in sentiment. In any given week, the chance that a long position in TER stock will be profitable is 54.04%. As it stands, bullish traders have a modest edge. Coming off the back of the 3-7-D sequence, these traders enjoy an extra boost in probability, thus incentivizing a debit-based strategy.

data.title: Five Below Q1 Earnings: Revenue, EPS Beat, Retailer Raises Guidance, CFO Will Step Down
data.description:
Five Below Inc FIVE reported first-quarter financial results Wednesday after the market close. Here’s a look at the key details from the quarter.

  • Q1 Revenue: $970.5 million, versus estimates of $966.49 million
  • Q1 Adjusted EPS: 86 cents, versus estimates of 82 cents

Total revenue was up 19.5% year-over-year, while comparable sales increased 7.1% year-over-year. The company reported operating income of $50.8 million in the first quarter. 

Five Below opened 55 new stores in the quarter, bringing total locations up to 1,826 at quarter's end. The company ended the period with $427.46 million in cash and cash equivalents.

“Our first quarter results demonstrate the effectiveness of our strategy, grounded in trend-right product, extreme value and a fun store experience. We were pleased to see broad-based strength across the majority of our merchandising worlds, resulting in a transaction-driven 7.1% increase in comparable sales, as well as strong performance from our new stores,” said Winnie Park, CEO of Five Below.

The company noted that CFO Kristy Chipman has decided to step down from her roles for personal reasons. COO Ken Bull will take on the role of interim CFO as the company searches for a replacement.

Outlook: Five Below expects second-quarter revenue in the range of $975 million to $995 million versus estimates of $958.31 million. The company anticipates second-quarter adjusted earnings of 50 cents to 62 cents per share versus estimates of 56 cents per share. 

Five Below also raised guidance for full-year 2025. The company sees full-year revenue of $4.33 billion to $4.42 billion, up from prior guidance of $4.21 billion to $4.33 billion. The company expects full-year adjusted earnings to be between $4.25 and $4.72 per share, up from prior guidance of $4.10 to $4.72 per share. Analysts currently anticipate full-year revenue of $4.37 billion and earnings of $4.76 per share, according to Benzinga Pro.

“Looking ahead, this unwavering focus on the core customer combined with disciplined execution of our strategy and the agility of our teams position us to deliver our financial and operational objectives as we navigate the impact of tariffs and the associated uncertainty in the current global trade environment,” Park said.

Five Below executives will further discuss the quarter on a call with investors and analysts at 4:30 p.m. ET.

FIVE Price Action: Five Below shares were up 0.63% in after-hours, trading at $123.83 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

  • Walmart Leverages Automation And AI To Cut Costs And Grow, Paving The Way For Other Retailers: Report

Photo: Retail Photographer/Shutterstock.

data.title: Mullen Stock Is Soaring Higher Wednesday: What's Going On?
data.description:
Mullen Automotive, Inc. MULN shares are trading higher on Wednesday after the company announced it entered into securities purchase agreements following its 1-for-100 reverse stock split.

What To Know: The company said it entered into two securities purchase agreements involving the sale of 5% original issue discount secured notes and five-year cash-exercisable warrants.

Under the first agreement, Mullen issued about $11.6 million in notes and warrants to purchase 1.65 million shares of common stock.

In the second agreement, the company issued approximately $2.8 million in notes and warrants for 387,969 shares.

Both agreements involve notes that are convertible into common stock, with all terms adjusted to reflect the reverse stock split effective June 2.

MULN Price Action: Mullen shares closed Wednesday 198.70% higher at $16.10, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image: Courtesy of Mullen Automotive, Inc.

data.title: Micron Technology Unusual Options Activity For June 04
data.description:
Investors with a lot of money to spend have taken a bullish stance on Micron Technology MU.

And retail traders should know.

We noticed this today when the trades showed up on publicly available options history that we track here at Benzinga.

Whether these are institutions or just wealthy individuals, we don't know. But when something this big happens with MU, it often means somebody knows something is about to happen.

So how do we know what these investors just did?

Today, Benzinga's options scanner spotted 55 uncommon options trades for Micron Technology.

This isn't normal.

The overall sentiment of these big-money traders is split between 52% bullish and 34%, bearish.

Out of all of the special options we uncovered, 14 are puts, for a total amount of $2,991,975, and 41 are calls, for a total amount of $3,128,028.

What's The Price Target?

Taking into account the Volume and Open Interest on these contracts, it appears that whales have been targeting a price range from $60.0 to $220.0 for Micron Technology over the last 3 months.

Insights into Volume & Open Interest

Examining the volume and open interest provides crucial insights into stock research. This information is key in gauging liquidity and interest levels for Micron Technology's options at certain strike prices. Below, we present a snapshot of the trends in volume and open interest for calls and puts across Micron Technology's significant trades, within a strike price range of $60.0 to $220.0, over the past month.

Micron Technology Call and Put Volume: 30-Day Overview

Biggest Options Spotted:

About Micron Technology

Micron is one of the largest semiconductor companies in the world, specializing in memory and storage chips. Its primary revenue stream comes from dynamic random access memory, or DRAM, and it also has minority exposure to not-and or NAND, flash chips. Micron serves a global customer base, selling chips into data centers, mobile phones, consumer electronics, and industrial and automotive applications. The firm is vertically integrated.

Current Position of Micron Technology

  • RSI indicators hint that the underlying stock may be overbought.
  • Next earnings are expected to be released in 21 days.

data.title: What's Going On With Reddit Shares Today?
data.description:
Reddit Inc. RDDT shares are trading higher Wednesday after the company filed a lawsuit against artificial intelligence startup Anthropic, accusing it of unauthorized use of Reddit's data for AI model training.

What To Know: According to the complaint filed in California, Reddit claims Anthropic accessed its site more than 100,000 times even after stating it had stopped scraping data.

The lawsuit alleges Anthropic trained its AI models, including the recently released Claude Opus 4, using Reddit content without a license or user consent, despite Reddit having formal data licensing deals with OpenAI and Google, according to The Wall Street Journal.

Reddit argues its data is particularly valuable due to the human-to-human conversational format across over 100,000 subreddits. The company says it previously attempted to reach a licensing agreement with Anthropic but those talks failed. Reddit also points to a 2021 Anthropic research paper that specifically cited Reddit data as useful for training AI systems.

The stock's move reflects investor optimism that Reddit is taking steps to protect and monetize its data assets in the AI space.

RDDT Price Action: Reddit shares closed Wednesday up 6.63% at $118.21, according to Benzinga Pro.
